rulings.Case Title: GOLDEN PROPERTIES, LLC VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 3, 2023 line 1. PETITIONER GOLDEN PROPERTIES, LLC Motion To Set Aside Judgment is OFF CALENDAR. The Court adopts its tentative ruling issued for December 22, 2022 hearing. While it was suggested at December 22, 2022 hearing that the Court has jurisdiction to rule on the merits, the supplemental brief filed by the petitioner does not support this contention devoting 8 lines of citations and no analysis to this issue. (The Court notes that these citations add nothing on the issue of void judgment that had not been addressed in the moving memorandum of points authorities and that CCP 473(d) was no listed as grounds for the motion in the Notice of Motion filed on November 14, 2022). The Court did not have jurisdiction on December 22, 2022. Therefore, the original tentative ruling stands and the Motion to Set Aside judgment is OFF CALENDAR. Unauthorized reply filed on February 2, 2022 is stricken. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: OG TREE, INC. VS. OCEAN AVENUE REAL ESTATE FUND, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Relief From Dismissal Pursuant To 473(B); Memorandum Of Points & Authorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 3, 2023 line 2. PLAINTIFF OG TREE, INC. MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Relief From Dismissal Pursuant To 473(B) is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice per CCP 1005 and CCP 1003. Amended Motion signed on January 10, 2022, 5 days after the ex-parte application to continue the hearing to February 3, 2023 was granted to allow proper filing and service (See Yellin Declaration in support of January 5, 2023 ex-parte application, paragraphs 6 and 9). According to the proof of service on file the amended motion was served by mail on January 11, 2023 and the amendment motion was filed 7 days later on January 18, 2023. There are only 16 courts days between January 11, 2023 and February 3, 2023; and only 12 court days between January 18, 2023 and February 3, 2023. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
